raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Artifact makes a contribution claim (first systematic token-level recalibration study) and a gap statement; any general review must assess whether the framing and thesis are coherent and accurate.",
    "R1.2": "Cross-section consistency is at risk: the paper describes training on dev-other then testing on test-other without addressing potential leakage, and the logical chain from temperature scaling to the proposed extension needs coherence checking.",
    "R1.3": "Specific ECE and WER numbers are reported against named baselines; validating that those comparisons are internally consistent and that the figures cited (Figure 3) support the calibration narrative is central to any 'what do you think' review.",
    "R1.4": "The paper explicitly claims priority ('first systematic study'); assessing whether that delta over nearest prior work is accurately characterised is load-bearing regardless of review focus.",
    "R1.7": "Hannun et al. (2014) is labelled 'most recent confidence-aware ASR model', a description that is almost certainly inaccurate given the 12-year gap to submission; citation accuracy and coverage are materially at issue."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritise scientific validity — checking claim-evidence alignment, citation accuracy, and logical consistency (R1.2, R1.3, R1.7) — or novelty and contribution positioning — assessing whether the 'first systematic study' framing holds against the literature (R1.1, R1.4)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Artifact makes a contribution claim (first systematic token-level recalibration study) and a gap statement; any general review must assess whether the framing and thesis are coherent and accurate.
rationale.R1.2: Cross-section consistency is at risk: the paper describes training on dev-other then testing on test-other without addressing potential leakage, and the logical chain from temperature scaling to the proposed extension needs coherence checking.
rationale.R1.3: Specific ECE and WER numbers are reported against named baselines; validating that those comparisons are internally consistent and that the figures cited (Figure 3) support the calibration narrative is central to any 'what do you think' review.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly claims priority ('first systematic study'); assessing whether that delta over nearest prior work is accurately characterised is load-bearing regardless of review focus.
rationale.R1.7: Hannun et al. (2014) is labelled 'most recent confidence-aware ASR model', a description that is almost certainly inaccurate given the 12-year gap to submission; citation accuracy and coverage are materially at issue.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritise scientific validity — checking claim-evidence alignment, citation accuracy, and logical consistency (R1.2, R1.3, R1.7) — or novelty and contribution positioning — assessing whether the 'first systematic study' framing holds against the literature (R1.1, R1.4)?
